underlying: Delaware U.S. Senate 2026 D-R margin in pp (Coons D incumbent vs GOP)
method: AUDIT FIX 2026-04-29: mu 22->24. Anchored on (a) Coons' 2014 (+13.8) and 2020 (+22.3 over Lauren Witzke) margins, (b) Delaware's deep-blue PVI (D+7), Harris won DE ~+15 in 2024, (c) D-favorable 2026 generic ballot, (d) weak GOP field (no Cabinet-tier challenger). Audit raised mu from 22 to 24: incumbent advantage + D-friendly midterm + weak GOP field together imply modest outperformance vs Coons' 2020 +22.3 mark. Sigma 6pp because zero public polls yet.
notes: Coons (D incumbent) won 2020 with 59.4-37.1 (+22.3pp); Blunt Rochester's 2024 open-seat win was tighter at +14.5 in a R-trending year. Mu=+22 anchors on Coons' personal 2020 mark, accepting some Trump-era erosion offset by midterm anti-incumbency on R. Note Kalshi strikes start at P14 — even Blunt Rochester's 2024 result clears this — implying tail risk skewed toward higher buckets.
